Ad Text:
About the role




We’re looking for a Strategy & Planning Manager to play a key role in connecting Hanwha Energy Australia’s HQ in Korea with its Australian energy retail business (Nectr).

You’ll work closely with the leadership and retail teams, helping turn corporate strategy into practical actions while ensuring clear, accurate insights flow back to senior leaders in Korea. This is a highly visible role that sits across strategy, financial planning, and business performance, with exposure to executive decision‑making both locally and internationally.

If you enjoy working across cultures, turning complex information into clear insights, and influencing direction in a fast‑moving energy business, this role offers both challenge and impact.





What you’ll do

Be the main point of contact between Korean HQ and the Australian retail business

Coordinate with Trading, Sales and senior leadership to stay aligned on priorities

Help translate group strategy into local business plans and execution

Pull together insights across the business, including:

Market and regulatory changes

Commercial and sales performance

Operational delivery and compliance

Prepare high‑quality reports and updates for HQ and senior stakeholders

Build and maintain financial models for budgeting, forecasting and performance tracking

Perform scenario and sensitivity analysis to support strategic decisions

Use SQL to extract and analyse operational and financial data

Validate and consolidate data for reporting and reviews

Look for ways to streamline reporting and improve data processes




About you

7–9 years’ experience in strategy, planning, finance or analytics (energy sector strongly preferred)

Strong financial modelling and Excel skills

Hands‑on experience using SQL for data analysis

Comfortable working with senior leaders across functions and countries

Clear, structured communicator with strong problem‑solving skills

Fluent English; Korean language skills highly regarded




Preferred

Experience in energy retail or energy trading

Understanding of the Australian electricity market and regulatory environment

Background working in a multinational or HQ–local operating model

Familiarity with retail energy metrics such as pricing, margins, churn and load profile

Company: Department of Primary Industries & Regional Development
Location: Orange, Blue Mountains & Central West NSW (Hybrid)
Category:
Business/Systems Analysts (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Fixed term 6 month contract (with possibility of extension/ongoing employment)
Hybrid work environment on offer – location negotiable across regional NSW
Salary ranging from $129,464pa - $142,665pa + super
Create impact in your industry, using your specialist skills and experience

As an ICT Business Analyst in our Digital & Data team, you’ll be a key driver of digital transformation partnering with the ICT Senior Project Officer and wider ICT team to uncover what the business truly needs, shape fit-for-purpose solutions and help deliver projects from concept through to implementation. You’ll get to work on a group-wide, cutting-edge CRM project (among other major initiatives), helping streamline customer interactions, lift service quality, and enable better decision-making. If you enjoy tackling complex problems, bringing people together, and turning insights into clear requirements that teams can build from, we’d love to hear from you.

About the Team
Local Land Service (LLS) is a regional-focused NSW Government agency delivering quality customer services to farmers, landholders and the wider community. We help people make better decisions about the land they manage and assist rural and regional communities to be profitable and sustainable into the future.

Your day-to-day duties:

Analyse end-to-end business processes, pinpoint opportunities, and help design and deliver practical improvements that increase efficiency and service quality.
Partner with stakeholders to gather, validate and interpret data, turning it into insights and recommendations that support confident decisions and measurable outcomes.
Juggle competing priorities across multiple intersecting projects and ICT systems—staying across dependencies, risks and delivery impacts.
Create and maintain clear, useful artefacts (process maps, requirements, business rules, SOPs) that support delivery and continuous improvement.

What you’ll bring:

Strong analytical skills and the ability to translate business needs into clear technical requirements.
Experience in stakeholder management, process mapping, and documentation, with the ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical users.
A background in business analysis, with a solid understanding of ICT systems and data analysis, will be essential to drive process improvements and ensure successful project outcomes.

Key knowledge and experience:

Extensive experience in technical business analysis including aid in solution development
Demonstrated experience with data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, BI tools or similar and strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience in process modelling and improvement methodologies.

Essential requirements:

Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Computer Science, Project Management, or related discipline, and/or equivalent experience
A current NSW driver licence combined with the willingness and ability to travel across NSW (including overnight stays if required) is also required.

For more information on this role, you can download the  role description.

Want more information? Contact Caroline Jones | Senior Manager ICT Solutions at caroline.a.jones@lls.nsw.gov.au.

Applying is Easy! 

To apply, please submit a copy of your resume (approx. 4 pages) and a cover letter (approx. 2 pages) that tells us about your relevant skills and experience and how these make you an ideal candidate for this role. 

What is the Process?  

The process starts with you submitting your application. Once your application has been received, you will receive an email confirmation and further correspondence when the role advertisement closes. 

You might be asked to attend an interview (either via video, over the phone or face to face) or complete a written or other assessment task.

Application Closing Date:  Sunday 12th April 2026 at 11.55pm 

Recruitment Adjustments 

If you require adjustments in completing your application, interviewing, completing any pre- employment testing, require information in an alternative format, or otherwise participating in the recruitment process, please let us know so we can assist. Send an email to talent.advisory@dpird.nsw.gov.au or contact the hiring manager listed above for a confidential discussion. 

About Us: Together, we are DPIRD

The New South Wales Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development (DPIRD) protects, supports and develops primary industries and regional economies.

Collectively we support strong and sustainable agriculture, forestry, fishing and mining industries, and regional economies. We actively manage our natural assets: our lands and soils, minerals, waterways and oceans, vegetation and forests.

The department brings together:

Local Land Services
NSW Resources
Agriculture and Biosecurity
Fisheries and Forestry
Regional Development and Delivery.

With nearly 5,000 employees, and 75 per cent of the DPIRD team living and working in regional NSW, we are embedded in the regions we serve.

Diversity and inclusion are core DPIRD values. Everyone has the right to contribute and participate to their full potential. We are committed to supporting a diverse and inclusive culture that is respectful, welcoming and flexible – a workplace where our people feel supported, valued, and part of our collective success.

Learn more about the benefits of joining our diverse and talented DPIRD team.

Additional Information

A recruitment pool may be created for ongoing and temporary roles of the same role or role type that may become available for filling over the next eighteen months. 

The successful applicant in this role will be required to undergo reference checks, National Police Check, and Bankruptcy Check.

“It begins at Bendigo Bank”

 

We’ve never been ‘just a bank’. Just like you should never be ‘just an employee’. We’re united in our belief that in banking, better can be bigger, and together we’re making it happen.

 

It starts here. With Bendigo Bank… and you.



About the role

This is a pivotal opportunity to go beyond traditional oversight and take ownership of our Group's Conduct Risk framework. More than just a policy-holder, you will be a trusted advisor to our most senior leaders, using your expertise to influence key decisions and shape our approach to risk across the entire organisation.

If you are a seasoned risk professional with a passion for driving meaningful change and the credibility to influence at an executive level, this is your chance to play a crucial leadership role in our journey.

Your responsibilities will include:

Own the Framework: Take full ownership of the design, implementation, and ongoing effectiveness of the Group's Conduct Risk Policy, ensuring it is embedded and effective across all business divisions.
Act as a Strategic Advisor: Serve as the key subject matter expert on conduct risk, providing critical insights and influential advice to senior management committees and key governance forums.
Drive Data-Led Insights: Develop and manage sophisticated risk reporting, including data stewardship for our conduct risk appetite and key risk indicators, to ensure accurate and actionable intelligence.
Lead with Influence: Proactively identify and lead targeted deep-dive reviews. Although you won't have direct reports, you will be a core leader within the team, mentoring junior members and spearheading initiatives to uplift culture and capability.
Collaborate for Success: Work closely with a wide range of senior stakeholders across all three Lines of Accountability to ensure a cohesive and robust approach to managing conduct risk.



 This is a permanent, full-time opportunity, ideally located at our Melbourne office, but we are open to Sydney, or Adelaide for the right person. We believe in putting our people first, which is why we offer flexible, hybrid work options with a minimum of 3 days in the office per week.

 


About you

We're looking for a passionate go-getter with:

Experience in the Banking and Finance sector, bringing a strong background in Conduct Risk, non-financial risk, and compliance frameworks.
Proven ability to operate and influence at the most senior levels, with exceptional stakeholder management skills and experience partnering strategically with Executive and Senior Management.
Demonstrable experience in designing and implementing practical risk frameworks and leading large-scale projects or change programs from a strategic and practical standpoint.
A strategic and critical thinker with outstanding problem-solving skills, who thrives in a complex and agile environment, effectively managing competing priorities and driving continuous improvement.



 

So, why work with us?

You’ll also get access to a great range of benefits, including:

Looking for hybrid work, flexible hours, or a compressed week? Our flexible work options are designed to fit your lifestyle.
Health and well-being support, including discounted gym memberships, private health insurance options, and our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for you and your immediate family members.
Take your learning to the next level through opportunities like our corporate university ‘BEN U’ or at an external provider of your choice.


We believe a diverse workforce supported by an inclusive culture is central to our success and we actively encourage applications from those who bring diversity of thought to our business. We support candidate requests for adjustment to accommodate an illness, injury, or disability to equitably participate in the selection process.


How to apply 

If you are interested in this opportunity, please apply online with your CV and cover letter.

PD:  Senior Manager Conduct Risk (Enterprise Risk) - PD.docx

Applications Close: Sunday 5 April 2026. Shortlisting and interviews may take place prior to the advertised close date, so don't delay: apply now!

About DBG Health & Axe Health Services:   
At DBG Health, we are Defined by Good. Guided by a simple but powerful purpose: to create a world where feeling better is a right, not a privilege. Every day, millions of people rely on us for access to affordable, innovative health, wellness, and beauty solutions that make a genuine impact. Headquartered in Melbourne, we are a global-scale, privately owned organisation, powered by over 1,500 passionate professionals, united by a mission to help people feel better, live well, and thrive.   
 
Axe Health Services, a subsidiary of DBG Health, delivers a full spectrum of strategic solutions tailored for pharmacies and the pharmaceutical industry. Our unified service platforms provide a streamlined and cohesive multi-channel approach to health service implementation through our business' units, which include PharmaPrograms, Farmaforce, Sinapse Health, Praxhub, Wellness, and Precision Health. 
 
About The Role
 
The Senior Health Strategist at Sinapse Health is responsible for managing the delivery of External consulting, advisory, and analytical projects that support operational improvement, commercial decision-making, and strategic planning across the organisation.
 
Reporting to the General Manager, Sinapse Health, this role oversees project execution, ensures high-quality insights, and drives measurable outcomes for internal stakeholders. The position leads small consulting teams, coordinates cross-functional contributors, and ensures project recommendations are practical, evidence-based, and aligned with organisational priorities.
 
Role & Responsibilities  

Design and deliver evidence-based projects, including diagnostics, analysis, and recommendations that drive business outcomes.
Oversee multiple projects with clear planning, stakeholder alignment, risk management, and timely delivery.
Build trusted relationships, facilitate workshops, and translate complex insights into clear recommendations for senior leaders.
Identify and implement opportunities to enhance operational, financial, and service performance.
Standardise methodologies and tools - Develop and apply frameworks, templates, and processes to ensure consistent, high-quality delivery.
Maintain adherence to policies and safety standards while actively building capability and industry knowledge.
Skills & Experience  

Experience in diagnostics, financial analysis, and developing strategic, evidence-based recommendations.
Ability to interpret complex data and translate insights into clear, actionable outcomes.
Strong presentation skills with the ability to engage stakeholders and simplify complex concepts.
Understanding of healthcare operating models, financial drivers, and service delivery frameworks.
Proven ability to manage multiple projects, prioritise effectively, mitigate risks, and drive stakeholder alignment.
Background in consulting, healthcare or related sectors, with experience delivering impact in fast-paced, complex environments and supporting team development.
Benefits    

Exclusive employee discounts via the DBG Health Employee Shop, including MCoBeauty, Esmi Skin Minerals, Nude by Nature, e.l.f. Cosmetics, Chemist's Own and more.  
Acacia EAP Wellbeing Support, providing confidential 24/7 counselling, mental health support, financial coaching, and manager assistance.  
Join a values-driven, supportive, and friendly culture within a dynamic, fast-growing business that actively promotes professional development and internal mobility.  
Enjoy the ability to work autonomously in an empowering environment that supports ownership, accountability and personal success.  
Access to the Bastas Academy for Health Leadership (via Melbourne Business School), supporting emerging and senior leaders with industry-leading programs, coaching, and development.  
DBG Health Leadership Academy, offering structured leadership development, mentoring, and clear career progression pathways.  
DBG Health is committed to fostering a workplace culture that is safe and accessible for all. We are an equal- opportunity employer and encourage applications regardless of cultural background, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. We strive to build a collaborative, inclusive workplace that is representative of the communities we work and live in.

At TechnologyOne, we're partnering with Local Government clients on exciting new projects that help modernise essential services, improve operational efficiency, and deliver better outcomes for communities.

As Solution Industry Lead, you will play a critical role in leading the successful implementation of our solutions, guiding customers through complex transformation projects from design through to go live. This is a highly visible role where you'll combine delivery leadership, customer partnership, and commercial thinking to drive project success. If you enjoy leading meaningful programs, building trusted relationships, and helping customers realise value, this is a strong opportunity to make an impact.

What you'll be doing…


Lead the end-to-end implementation of TechnologyOne solutions across Local Government projects, including solution design, configuration, user testing, and go-live guidance
Provide direction to Project Managers and key stakeholders on deployment phasing, integrations, governance, and implementation methodology
Partner closely with customers to understand their business, shape delivery strategies, and set projects up for success
Drive a strong customer experience through clear communication, trusted stakeholder engagement, and consistent delivery against commitments
Proactively identify, manage, and escalate risks to minimise impact on customer outcomes and overall project success
Continually challenge the status quo to improve processes, systems, efficiency, and delivery outcomes while maintaining compliance with policies and WHS requirements

Your Talents…


Proven ability to lead complex solution implementations and deliver strong customer outcomes in a project-based environment
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build credibility and work effectively with customers, Project Managers, and internal teams
Commercially minded, with sound judgement and an ability to balance customer needs, delivery quality, and project success
Confident navigating risk, governance, and escalation points in a way that keeps projects moving and relationships strong
A continuous improvement mindset, with the ability to identify opportunities to enhance delivery, efficiency, and overall impact
Experience working within, or delivering solutions to, Local Government environments will be highly regarded

Benefits…


Competitive remuneration package
Access to our industry leading employee share plan
Salary packaging options
Up to 10 days of additional leave via our Purchase Leave program
Amazing Culture: 8 x Australian Business Awards "Employer of Choice" winner
We love our team events & celebrate diversity
Flexible hours: we are family-friendly and value life outside of work
Free gym membership in your region (or, if you are located at HQ, we have a new state of the art Gym onsite, exclusive and available 24/7).
Additional 2.5 days of leave per year dedicated to volunteering at a charity of your choice
Free breakfast and coffee on-site
Accelerate your growth with full access to LinkedIn Learning and a clear career framework that maps your path to success.

Why Join us?

TechnologyOne is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity and inclusion. We offer competitive salaries, clear career progression, and a supportive team environment.

Our culture encourages creativity, innovation, and ownership, and our people enjoy a range of benefits and opportunities to get involved in community initiatives.

Who we are… TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE) is Australia's largest enterprise software company and an ASX 50 business, operating in six countries.

Our SaaS ERP platform powers over 1,200 organisations, including corporations, government agencies, councils, and universities, helping them simplify operations while staying ahead of technology.

Job Title: Senior Finance Business Partner - Margin and Marketing
Ad Text:
Stake is looking for a Senior Finance Business Partner - Margin and Marketing to join our wonderful Finance team, at our brand new CBD office, within a flexible hybrid working model!

We’re an Australian investing platform that helps ambitious people confidently grow their wealth. By offering seamless, immersive access to the share markets, we’ve empowered a new wave of investors. Stake has grown fast from a fintech startup into a robust financial services company. Today we have 750,000+ customers and more than A$5 billion under administration.

At Stake, we break barriers in everything we do. Just like our customers, we’re driven by ambition. Our desire for continuous growth is what creates the collaborative and high-performance environment we work in. Though we’re spread around the world, we’re a unified team moving towards a shared vision: to unleash the investor in everyone.

Join us as we continue to reshape the financial industry, inspiring investor confidence and celebrating our wins along the way.

About this role


This role is embedded in the business to support teams to ensure every marketing dollar and product decision is measured against clear financial outcomes and optimised to ensure we are making the right commercial decisions for Stake. Trade, Stake Super, Save and Rewards each have different economics and growth levers; this role provides the unifying finance lens, — CAC and channel economics, value attribution & incrementality, wholesale/COGS governance, decision support, forecast and financial insight generation to help guide future outcomes — so the business grows profitably and cost drivers are understood and controlled.

You will be joining our high-performing Finance team who enables and supports our Australia, New Zealand, India and Brazil operations during an exciting hyper-growth phase. Opportunity to work in a truly customer-centric environment where innovative and seamless products and services are evolving the way Australians and Kiwis invest.

Further responsibilities include:

Embedded partnership: Act as a dedicated finance partner to Marketing and Product, translating financials into simple insights and helping teams understand key drivers of margin, LTV, and performance.
Data-driven insights: Use customer, marketing, and financial data to identify opportunities (e.g. cohorts, funnel performance, unit economics) and recommend optimisations.
Commercial challenge: Provide constructive challenge to plans and assumptions, highlighting risks, trade-offs, and improving decision quality.
Decision enablement: Empower teams to move quickly by setting financial guardrails, KPIs, and decision frameworks that balance speed with control.
Forecasting & planning: Own forecasting for marketing and margins, including scenario analysis and long-term planning to guide investment decisions.
Financial storytelling: Translate performance into clear narratives explaining results, drivers, and recommended actions for stakeholders and leadership.
Prioritisation & allocation: Partner with Product, Marketing and commercial leadership to prioritise the roadmap and allocate resources (headcount, budgets etc) based on quantified financial impact, marginal ROI and strategic fit. Build simple prioritisation frameworks and decision matrices, run trade-off analysis, and recommend resource shifts that maximise margin, LTV/CAC outcomes and improved financial return.
Reporting & data integrity: Build and maintain a reliable single source of truth for financial and performance metrics with strong automation.
Coaching and Capability building: Build commercial acumen across the business and upskill the finance team to uplift commercial literacy and adoption of finance frameworks.
A bit about you

5+ years in FP&A, commercial finance, or marketing finance with experience embedded in growth/marketing teams (fintech, marketplace, consumer finance or high-growth tech preferred).
Strong commercial modelling capability to be able to provide decision support and scenario analysis.
Demonstrated experience building CAC/LTV frameworks, channel and product attribution, and translating them into business decisions.
Deep commercial curiosity, ability to simplify complex models and teach non-finance stakeholders.
Strong communication & storytelling skills with experience presenting to senior marketing/product leaders as well as C-suite.
Comfortable working with Data/Analytics and writing technical requirements for building a reliable Single source of truth for use by Finance and beyond. Strong analytical toolkit: advanced Excel, SQL advantageous, and experience with Hex beneficial.

At Stake, we’re more interested in your attitude than your resume. If you believe this is a role you want to jump into, please apply and let us know why in your cover letter!


Benefits of working at Stake


You’ll have the opportunity to work with autonomy while learning from some of the best talent in the industry, playing a meaningful role in building a company that has loads of positive momentum and excitement around it. Having said that, we also have perks.

Please see on our Careers website the list of benefits under our Grow with Stake program. They include:

Career relevant development and training with professional development allowance
Up to 16 weeks of paid parental leave
Childcare allowance
Sonder - healthcare, safety support and EAP for you and your immediate family
Additional leave types (Swap days, Bear Days, TOIL, Compassionate, Sorry Business)
Employee Share Options scheme for senior leaders
Stake products: SMSF 30% discount on yearly membership fee, $295 AUD annual trading credit and Stake Black account
Recognition awards and team celebrations
Discretionary performance bonus and talent referral bonus

Stake is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity. We’re proactive about providing an inclusive work environment and do not discriminate based on race, religion, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, age or disability status. We’re also endorsed by WORK180 as a great employer for women.

It all comes together to explain why Stake placed 1st in the 2024 AFR BOSS Best Places to Work list for the Banking, Superannuation & Financial Services category, across all of Australia and New Zealand.


So what happens next?


Once we've received your application, we'll give it the attention it deserves and you'll hear from us either way. We’re committed to responding to every applicant. If we think you’d be a good addition to our team, we’ll give you a call and from there, you'll cruise through our recruitment process.

To all recruitment agencies: Stake does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias or directly to Stake employees. Stake is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:


Help shape the future of Australia's largest UKG practice
Join a national community of 90+ UKG specialists within Deloitte's Human Capital practice
Work on complex workforce transformation programs with some of Australia's most recognised organisations

About the Team

Deloitte's UKG practice is the largest in Australia and continues to grow rapidly. Our team brings together specialists who design and deliver workforce technology solutions that help organisations better manage, schedule and support their people.

Joining Deloitte means working on large-scale UKG implementations that transform how organisations operate. Our teams deliver across industries including health, retail, supply chain, hospitality and the public sector, solving complex workforce challenges and enabling organisations to operate more effectively.

You'll work across the full UKG ecosystem, supported by specialists in timekeeping, advanced scheduling, forecasting, analytics, reporting and integrations.

What sets this team apart is the depth of expertise around you. With more than 90 UKG specialists across Australia, and access to Deloitte's global UKG community supporting hundreds of clients worldwide, you'll join a genuine community of practice where collaboration, knowledge sharing and continuous learning are central to how we work.

What will your typical day look like?

As a Specialist Lead within our Workforce Payroll Solutions team, you will play a key role delivering UKG Pro Workforce Management (WFM) implementation programs for leading organisations.

You'll combine deep product expertise with consulting capability to design and deliver workforce technology solutions that integrate seamlessly within a client's broader HR and payroll technology landscape.

Your responsibilities will include:

Designing and architecting UKG Pro WFM solutions aligned to client business needs
Facilitating client workshops to capture and translate workforce management requirements
Advising on best use of UKG functionality, integrations and user experience
Configuring and supporting the implementation of UKG WFM modules
Guiding clients through complex workforce design decisions
Collaborating with project teams to deliver solutions aligned to project timelines
Mentoring team members and contributing to capability development across the UKG practice
Staying up to date with the latest UKG releases, innovations and certifications

Our teams typically work across one to three projects at a time, allowing you to focus on delivering meaningful outcomes and high-quality solutions for clients.

Purpose & Impact

Our work combines technology, business insight and practical delivery to help organisations transform how they manage and support their workforce.

In this role, you will:

Deliver complex UKG implementation programs that improve workforce operations and employee experience
Work with leading organisations across health, retail, supply chain, hospitality and government
Contribute to end-to-end workforce transformation alongside specialists in payroll, HCM, workforce integrity, organisational design and change management
Help shape the future of Australia's largest UKG practice

People & Culture

You'll be part of a national community of more than 90 UKG specialists who collaborate openly and support each other's success.

Our team brings together functional consultants, developers and transformation specialists, creating an environment where people learn from each other and solve complex problems together.

You can expect:

A strong knowledge-sharing culture supported by communities of practice
Regular learning sessions and team connection events
Collaboration across the broader Deloitte Human Capital practice

Learning & Growth

This role offers the opportunity to build deep expertise in workforce technology while developing a broader consulting career. Deloitte invests heavily in capability development, innovation and knowledge sharing across the practice.

You will:

Learn from some of Australia's most experienced UKG specialists
Build expertise across the full UKG ecosystem including integrations, reporting and DataHub
Gain experience across multiple industries and large-scale transformation programs
Contribute to innovation initiatives including accelerators and AI-enabled workforce solutions

Enough about us — let's talk about you

You enjoy working at the intersection of workforce operations, technology and consulting.

You bring strong expertise in UKG Pro Workforce Management and enjoy partnering with clients to solve complex workforce challenges and deliver practical, scalable solutions.

What you'll bring

Proven experience working with UKG Pro Workforce Management (WFM) as a lead consultant or practitioner.
Experience translating business requirements into functional workforce technology solutions
Specialist knowledge in one or more WFM areas such as:
Time & Attendance
Optimised Rostering
Forecasting
Activities
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Demonstrated problem-solving and decision-making capability
Ability to manage multiple priorities across client delivery projects
Strong attention to detail and commitment to high-quality delivery
Tertiary qualifications in Business, HR, IT or related disciplines (advantageous)

Why Deloitte?  

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   
 We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  
We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

About the Company
A well-established organisation within the property sector is expanding its Group Finance team. The business provides specialist advice that supports investment and commercial decision-making across Australia. This growth reflects the organisation’s continued expansion and a number of strategic initiatives currently underway.

About the Role
This newly created Commercial Analyst position offers broad exposure across the business. You will work on strategic projects, M&A activity, and financial planning and analysis, providing insights and support to senior stakeholders. This is a dynamic role designed to give a variety of experience and the opportunity to influence key business decisions.

Key Duties

Develop and maintain financial models to support investment decisions and strategic initiatives
Conduct scenario and sensitivity analysis to assess commercial risks and opportunities
Support strategic projects with ad-hoc modelling and analysis
Interpret complex financial information and prepare clear recommendations
Present findings to senior stakeholders and executive leadership
About You
You are commercially minded, analytical, and curious, with a strong technical grounding in accounting and a clear understanding of P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow. You will have 5–7 years’ experience, starting in accounting and progressing into a commercially focused role, most likely at the Commercial Analyst or FP&A Analyst level. You are proactive, adaptable, and confident, engaging with senior stakeholders, with strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and clear communication.

Skills & Experience

Experience in commercial finance, business analysis, or financial analysis roles
Exposure to M&A lifecycle activities highly regarded
CA/CPA qualified
Advanced Excel and PowerPoint capability

Job Title: Project Lead, AI Operations | 3 month FTC
Ad Text:
Terrific opportunity for a Strategic Project Lead, AI Operations to step into a brand new and fast moving function supporting the rollout of AI across a cutting edge global business. The role focuses on establishing operational cadence, aligning multiple departments, and embedding decentralised AI workflows across the business. This position acts as the “connective tissue” between departments to ensure consistency in execution, communication, and progress against broader AI strategy.
This three month FTC needs for someone who can quickly understand the landscape, build momentum, and help establish an operating rhythm from the ground up.

About the role
You will act as the key connector between departments, helping align strategy, systems, workflows, and communication across several teams working at different stages of AI adoption.
This is not a traditional project role. You will be expected to think across systems and processes, identify blockers, challenge where needed, and push cross functional work forward. 

Key Responsibilities

Define and launch the AI operations process; establish cadence, governance, and communication rhythm.
Run regular alignment sessions with 3 to 4 departments progressing at different stages of AI adoption.
Identify key stakeholders and establish clear communication and decision pathways.
Identify technical or structural blockers and collaborate with subject matter experts to resolve.
Facilitate access, training, and cross department connectors between systems.
Design, test, and improve interdepartmental connectors (APIs, automations, shared workflows).
Push project timelines forward and ensure teams deliver multi department updates on schedule.
Promote a unified communication style across teams through informal engagement sessions (e.g. lunch and learns).
Share success stories, learnings, and improvement opportunities across technical and non technical groups.
Support departmental embedded engineers and ensure they can execute their part of the AI strategy.
Identify skill gaps and coordinate with other teams to bridge them.
Ideal Background

Experienced in AI driven process design, automation, or operational project leadership.
Background in business analysis or systems and process mapping, able to think across technical and business contexts.
Experience working within fast paced tech environments.
Hands on understanding of connectors, workflows, and APIs (out of the box solutions vs. custom built).
Demonstrated experience in building or enhancing AI workflows, ideally with examples of tools or solutions personally developed or configured.
Personal Attributes

Adaptable, comfortable with ambiguity and fast changing priorities.
Confident challenging stakeholders constructively and keeping momentum despite obstacles.
Collaborative communicator who can engage both technical and non technical audiences.
Background in business analysis, operational coordination, or project delivery in a fast paced global environment.
Exposure to AI tools, workflow automation, or connected systems.
Experience working with cross functional teams and multiple stakeholders.
Ability to assess technical blockers and work through them with the right people.
Experience in environments where you have had to push hard to keep projects moving.
In this role, success will mean quickly understanding the current landscape, building trust across teams, creating structure where needed, and helping drive AI related initiatives forward with clarity and momentum.
If you are a hands on Project Leader / Business Analyst who enjoys bringing structure to new initiatives and working across teams to make things happen, please apply via the link or email jqiugley@sbrecruitment.com

Ad Text:
About Liontown

Liontown Limited (ASX: LTR) is one of Australia's most exciting emerging lithium producers. Our Kathleen Valley Lithium Project in Western Australia is in active production ramp-up, supplying high-quality spodumene concentrate to global battery supply chains. As we grow from developer to producer, we're building the team and capability to match our ambition.

The Opportunity

We're looking for an experienced IR professional to own and lead Liontown's investor relations function. You'll be the primary interface between our Executive Leadership Team and the investment community — shaping how domestic and international capital markets understand and value our story.

Reporting directly to the CFO, you'll work closely with the CEO/MD, Company Secretary, and Board, and play a central role in all capital markets activity as Liontown continues to grow.

What You'll Do

Develop and execute Liontown's IR strategy, aligned with corporate and capital markets objectives.
Build and maintain relationships with institutional investors, retail shareholders, and equity analysts domestically and internationally.
Lead investor engagement programs including roadshows, conferences, site visits, and results briefings.
Support continuous disclosure obligations — drafting and coordinating ASX announcements, quarterly reports, results materials, and investor presentations.
Monitor and analyse the investment community's perceptions of Liontown, including shifts in institutional interest, short positions, and shareholder register movements; provide regular reporting to ELT and Board.
Track equity research coverage and analyst models, maintaining constructive relationships with covering analysts to ensure accuracy and consistency with ASX announcements.
Monitor competitor activity, sector news, and macroeconomic developments relevant to the lithium and battery materials sector — synthesising intelligence and briefing leadership on emerging themes.
Own the IR digital presence including the Liontown website, LinkedIn, and shareholder communications.
Support capital markets transactions including equity raises, analyst days, and strategic investor processes — coordinating roadshow programs with corporate advisors, brokers, and custodian banks.
Prepare executive briefing packs, Q&A documents, and talking points ahead of investor-facing engagements.

What We're Looking For

Essential:

7+ years' experience in investor relations, equity capital markets, corporate advisory, or financial communications.
Demonstrated IR experience in an ASX-listed company, ideally resources, mining, or materials.
Strong working knowledge of ASX Listing Rules, continuous disclosure obligations, and ASIC guidance.
Credible communicator with institutional investors, analysts, and corporate advisors.
Outstanding written skills — able to translate complex financial and technical information into compelling narratives.

Highly Regarded:

Familiarity with the lithium, critical minerals, or battery materials sector.
Experience supporting capital markets transactions or major operational transitions.
Exposure to international IR programs and ESG reporting frameworks (TCFD, GRI).
CFA, MBA, or postgraduate qualification advantageous.

Why Liontown

Be part of a genuine growth story at a critical inflection point — production ramp-up, global interest, and strategic momentum.
Direct access to and influence with the CEO/MD, CFO, and Board.
Perth-based with domestic and international travel.
A values-led culture grounded in Safety, Integrity, Ambition, and Respect.

How to Apply

If you're ready to shape the investor story of one of Australia's most talked-about lithium producers, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply with your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience and what draws you to this role.

About Us

Wilmar Sugar Australia is part of Wilmar International, a leading global agribusiness with around 100,000 employees working across more than 30 countries and regions. 

Across Australia and New Zealand, we operate two major business units, Sugar & Renewables and Sweeteners, employing 2,400+ people during peak/ seasonal operations with corporate offices in Melbourne, Brisbane, Townsville and Auckland.

As Australia’s largest raw sugar manufacturer and a top 10 global producer, Wilmar Sugar & Renewables runs eight sugar mills across North Queensland. We are also one of the nation’s largest generators of renewable electricity from biomass, a major producer of bioethanol, the largest sugarcane grower, and a key supplier of sustainable fertiliser and stockfeed.

About the Role:

The Senior Commodity Price Risk Analyst/Trader is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and integrity of financial information provided for the Sugar & Renewables Group and providing support to the Risk Manager Sugar & Renewables to meet the commercial objectives of the business.

A key component of the role is to manage the day-to-day relationships with pricing banks & energy trading counterparties. Diligent execution of all daily processes involved in the aggregation and allocation of Grower Managed Sugar pricing orders with Wilmar’s Pricing Banks with a high degree of accuracy & autonomy is paramount. Reviewing overnight market price movements and bank fills along with an aptitude to challenge banks on pricing levels achieved, will also be required.

This is a Brisbane based, full-time office role.

Key Responsibilities

Manage complete daily grower pricing order end-to end processes, including order book aggregation through to order allocation to Wilmar’s Pricing Banks ahead of London/NY ICE market opening times.
Ensure Wilmar’s Commodity & FX pricing (MEI & GEI) is properly recorded and risk exposures accurately reported.
Collaborate with the Wilmar International Sugar & Grower marketing teams to develop grower analytical tools for insights into pricing trends and behaviours along with the development of new pool/individual pricing and product initiatives.
Provide Commercial support to the Risk Manager and the Group’s Cogeneration business through regular reporting of pricing positions & risk exposures for the Group’s Electricity & LGC hedging books.
Supporting the Risk Manager & Cogeneration Team to develop and maintain the Groups PPA & Energy Tariff models used for analysis for Electricity Export and Import load profiles across its eight factory locations. Wilmar’s Cogeneration business is Australia’s largest producer of renewable energy from Biomass.
Ensure accuracy and timeliness of financial information and ensure set deadlines are met and assist with the development and implementation of forecasting and budgeting.

About You

We are looking for someone who brings:

Bachelor Degree in Finance/Business/Commerce.
Minimum 5 years recent practical financial and/or energy markets commercial experience in a risk analysis or trading support capacity.
Working background in large/complex business environment, working across multiple disciplines/departments.
Experience in Financial markets (Commodity, FX and/or Energy)
Strong understanding of financial hedging products used in Commodity & FX markets
Good communication & presentation skills

Why work for Wilmar?

We want people who take pride in their work, enjoy building connections, and value opportunities to grow their career in a dynamic, future-focused organisation.

You will be joining a high-performing, dynamic, committed and passionate team that values collaboration, energy and delivering results. 

As a Wilmar employee, you will enjoy: 

Salary Sacrificing & Packaging– Options for super, novated leases and other eligible benefits
Novated Vehicle Leasing (Fleetplus) – Flexible, affordable lease and maintenance options
Superannuation – 14.5% super guarantee contribution, exclusive member benefits through the Wilmar Super Plan
Corporate Discounts & Rewards– Savings on fuel, tech, travel, and major retail brands
Health & Wellbeing– EAP, Medibank Corporate Cover, and eyewear discounts
Travel & Lifestyle– Discounts on car hire, hotels, and flights to help you recharge
Recognition & Workplace Giving – Milestone service awards, Values Awards and Charity Giving options available

Please note that if you are moving forward in the process, Wilmar requires all employees to complete Drug & Alcohol testing and medical assessments.

Wilmar is an equal opportunity employer.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to join a major Government program delivering meaningful outcomes for the community through large-scale digital and business transformation. You will step into a senior Business Analyst role where you will help shape future-state processes, drive requirements definition, and support the successful delivery of complex IM/ICT initiatives in a stakeholder-heavy environment. It suits someone who can operate strategically, work confidently across business and technical teams, and bring structure, clarity, and momentum to a fast-moving program.




About the Role

In this role, you will take the lead on business analysis activities across a complex program environment, helping define current and future state processes and ensuring requirements are clearly documented and traceable.


Lead the development of As-Is and To-Be business process models
Oversee business requirements documentation across the project lifecycle
Translate business needs into functional and technical requirements
Facilitate workshops, requirement gathering sessions, and issue resolution meetings
Provide updates to program leadership on delivery, schedule, financials, and benefits
Build strong relationships across business, delivery, and technical stakeholder groups




About You

You are a senior Business Analyst with strong delivery discipline, sharp stakeholder skills, and the ability to bring order to complexity in large-scale digital programs.


Extensive Business Analyst experience across complex IM/ICT projects
Strong knowledge of business analysis frameworks, tools, and best practice
Proven ability to lead, influence, and mentor within program environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills at all levels
Excellent analytical and problem-solving capability
Highly organised, collaborative, and comfortable managing competing priorities




The Benefits

This role offers the chance to contribute to meaningful public sector outcomes in a flexible and inclusive working environment.


Flexible work options to support work-life balance
Opportunity to contribute to high-impact Queensland Government programs
Inclusive, values-led culture with a strong focus on respect and diversity

Job Title: Business Analyst

Location: Queensland Remote

Contract Type: 6 month contract

Rate: $800 – $850 inc Super




We are seeking Business Analysts to support the design, review and improvement of complex business processes and enterprise systems. These roles sit within a large, multi‑stakeholder environment and offer exposure to discovery, process optimisation, system enhancement and strategic review initiatives. 




The Role

As a Business Analyst, you will work alongside project managers, technical specialists and business leaders to:

Capture and analyse current‑state processes, systems and pain points
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder interviews across diverse user groups
Define and document future‑state processes, business and system requirements
Support workflow design, governance improvements and system enhancement initiatives
Translate complex information into clear, concise and actionable documentation
Contribute to solution options, recommendations and implementation artefacts
Support knowledge transfer through documentation, presentations and workshops

Strong background in business process analysis and requirements definition
Proven ability to engage effectively with senior and operational stakeholders

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Experience with enterprise platforms, integrations or data‑enabled systems
Familiarity with BPMN or similar process modelling techniques
Background in regulated, public sector or higher education environments

As a Senior Data Modeller you will champion the enterprise data modelling practice, working closely with Data & Analytics teams (Data Analysts, Scientists, and Data Engineers), technology teams, and business stakeholders across the organization. You will own the enterprise data model strategy and architecture, establishing modelling standards and best practices that drive self-service analytics and promote standardization across all business domains. This senior role provides technical direction to Domain Data Modellers and serves as the authoritative subject matter expert for data modelling across the enterprise.

The core responsibilities of this role will be:

Drive strategy, design, and evolution of enterprise data models and data marts.
Optimize data architecture and query performance in cloud data warehouse environments.
Define and enforce enterprise-wide data modelling standards and methodologies.
Maintain modelling artifacts and frameworks to ensure consistency and quality.
Collaborate with Data Governance and Infrastructure teams on security and compliance.
Guide Domain Data Modellers to align with enterprise architecture.
Partner with business and analytics teams to define data requirements and modelling rules.
Translate complex business processes into scalable data structures.
Enhance cloud data warehouse, operational data stores, and metadata models.
Work with structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data to derive insights.
Operate autonomously at a senior level to mitigate project risks.

To be considered, key skills will include:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Management, or related field required
Cloud data platform certifications beneficial
7+ years hands-on experience with RDBMS platforms (e.g., SQL Server, Oracle, Teradata, etc.), relational and dimensional modelling in enterprise environments with demonstrated leadership in data modelling practice
Proven experience owning enterprise data architecture with responsibility for cross-functional data models and standards governance
Strong experience with modern cloud data platform environments (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, or similar) and data transformation frameworks (e.g., dbt) mandatory
Deep expertise in fact-dimensional modelling approaches and star/snowflake schema design patterns mandatory
Expert knowledge of metadata management, data modelling, and related tools (e.g., Erwin or ER Studio, SQL and/or PL/SQL) mandatory
Strong experience with modern cloud data platforms and data transformation frameworks
Deep expertise in dimensional modelling, star schema, and snowflake schema design
Ability to utilize Business Intelligence tools (e.g., Power BI or similar) to represent insights and validate data structures

 

Applications will close by the 16th of April

Shape how data and AI improve real-world outcomes for people and communities
Join an organisation investing heavily in technology, capability and innovation

Job Title: Head of Data Analytics and Insights
Ad Text:
Bolton Clarke is seeking an experienced Head of Data, Analytics & Insights to lead our enterprise data and analytics capability and enable evidence‑based decision‑making across the organisation.
This is a senior leadership role responsible for translating business strategy into trusted insights, modern data platforms and scalable analytics that directly improve service delivery, performance and customer outcomes.

About the role

Lead enterprise analytics, reporting and insight delivery (descriptive to predictive)
Embed advanced analytics, AI and automation into business processes
Partner with executives to define analytics priorities and measurable outcomes
Oversee data management, governance, quality, privacy and ethical use
Lead modern data and analytics platforms and advise on emerging technologies
Build and develop high‑performing analytics, data and BI teams
Champion data literacy and analytics maturity across the organisation
About you

Senior experience leading enterprise analytics and/or data functions
Strong stakeholder engagement and executive partnering capability
Proven delivery of data‑driven outcomes in complex organisations
Deep understanding of data governance, privacy and regulatory environments
Experience leading multidisciplinary technical teams
Exposure to advanced analytics, AI/ML and modern data platforms (highly desirable)
Tertiary qualifications in data, analytics, information systems or related fields are required. Postgraduate or executive leadership credentials are desirable.
About Us

Bolton Clarke is Australia’s largest independent, not‑for‑profit aged care provider shaping the future of positive ageing. With RSL Queensland and the Royal District Nursing Service at the heart of our DNA, we’ve cared for Australians since 1885. Today, our teams support more than 130,000 people to live independently at home and across our retirement living communities and residential aged care homes. Everything we do is dedicated to enabling, celebrating and supporting older Australians to age positively.

About our benefits:

Competitive base salary + $18,550 salary packaging benefits
Private health insurance discounts
Flight Centre Travel holiday deals
Flexible working environment
Employee Assistance Program
Fitness Passport Program
Free flu vaccinations
Importantly, you will mirror our values of Be Kind; Listen; Be Curious; Always safe; and Always together in all aspects of your work. 

How to Apply

To join our diverse, purpose-driven organisation that offers rewarding professional development opportunities, please apply below with a resume and cover letter.

Associate, Corporate Finance & Strategy | Avalon Beach, NSW (Hybrid)

 

About the Role

This is a high-impact finance role at the centre of a growing global hospitality group.

As Associate, Corporate Finance & Strategy, you'll work across financial planning, analysis, and strategic initiatives partnering with finance and operational leaders to deliver the forecasting, reporting, and insights that drive decision-making at every level of the business.

From monthly P&L and cash flow forecasting to Board reporting packs and investment analysis, your work will span the full breadth of Group finance. You'll also contribute to strategic projects, covenant tracking, scenario modelling, and continuous improvement including opportunities to lead AI adoption in financial processes.

What you'll do:

Support budgeting and long-range planning across the Group
Deliver monthly P&L, cash flow, and liquidity forecasting
Prepare financial analysis including budget vs. actuals commentary
Build reporting packs for leadership, Board, and investors
Support external reporting, covenant tracking, and funding analysis
Conduct scenario analysis and contribute to financial modelling
Play an active role in strategic planning and investment decisions
Drive process improvement and support AI adoption in finance

 

About You

You're commercially sharp, analytically strong, and comfortable translating financial data into clear, strategic insight. You communicate well across finance and non-finance audiences, bring genuine attention to detail, and thrive in a collaborative, fast-moving environment.

You'll bring:

4+ years’ experience in finance, consulting or related fields
Ideally combines strong technical grounding (e.g. audit, transaction services, consulting) with exposure to commercial or operational finance roles
CA / CPA qualification (highly regarded)
Strong command of financial statements, budgeting, and forecasting
Advanced Excel skills, including financial modelling
Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau (highly advantageous)
Sector experience in hospitality or travel (a plus, not a must)

 

About Us

Beckons is built around journeys of discovery for curious travelers seeking deeper connection to place, culture, and the natural world. Through a growing collection of lodges in some of the world’s most rare and remarkable destinations, the brand is guided by a regenerative approach to luxury travel, one that goes beyond sustainability to create positive, lasting impact for landscapes, wildlife, and the communities who call these places home.

 

The Beckons collection includes five award-winning properties in Australia, Southern Ocean Lodge on Kangaroo Island, Longitude 131° at Uluṟu-Kata Tjuṯa, Capella Lodge on Lord Howe Island, Silky Oaks Lodge at the Daintree Rainforest, The Louise in the Barossa Valley, while across the Tasman Sea in New Zealand lies the century-young Huka Lodge in Taupō. In the Americas, lodges include Clayoquot Wilderness Lodge on Canada’s Vancouver Island, and the wilds of Tierra Atacama and Tierra Patagonia in Chile.

Job Title: RFQ EL1 IM Policy, Governance & Compliance Lead
Ad Text:
Your new company

You'll be joining a specialist consulting environment working with a large, complex Australian Government agency in a data‑intensive context. The organisation is undertaking a significant uplift in its information and data management maturity, with a strong focus on legislative compliance, digital continuity and practical, staff‑friendly governance. This work sits at the intersection of policy, technology and operational reality, and is sponsored at senior executive level.

Your new role

As a senior Information Management / Records Management specialist, you will lead the development of contemporary, APS‑aligned IM policy and compliance artefacts and provide expert advice on records management capability across Microsoft 365.



Key responsibilities include:

Reviewing existing IM policy artefacts and conducting a structured gap analysis, including IM Policy, Normal Administrative Practice Policy, Digitise to Dispose Policy, IM Governance Framework and IM Strategy and Delivery Plan
Designing and authoring a new Information and Data Archival Policy and associated governance artefacts from first principles, aligned to the Archives Act 1983 and Privacy Act 1988
Producing clear, practical, plain‑English staff guidance for managing information in Outlook, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, network drives and Microsoft Purview
Ensuring all artefacts align with the Archives Act 1983, Privacy Act 1988, PSPF, NAA Digital Continuity 2025 and relevant APS standards
Facilitating policy review and endorsement sessions with governance, legal, enterprise architecture and cyber security stakeholders
Assessing SharePoint Online and Microsoft Purview against NAA records management requirements to identify gaps, risks and remediation options
Engaging with ICT Solution Front Door processes and IM, architecture, security and operations teams to validate technology feasibility
Delivering a structured Records Management Compliance Assessment Report with risk‑rated findings and recommendations
Contributing IM policy and compliance expertise to pilot lessons‑learned and compliance monitoring frameworks

What you'll need to succeed


To be successful in this role, you will bring:

7+ years' experience in APS information management, records management or IM compliance roles, ideally in health, statistics or other data‑intensive environments
Deep working knowledge of the Archives Act 1983, Privacy Act 1988, PSPF and NAA Digital Continuity 2025
Demonstrated experience authoring APS‑standard policies, governance frameworks and practical staff guidance that is genuinely adopted
Proven ability to conduct formal IM or records management compliance assessments with risk‑rated findings
Strong written communication skills, with experience producing SES‑facing material independently
Hands‑on understanding of SharePoint Online and Microsoft Purview from a governance and records compliance perspective
Familiarity with Microsoft Purview retention and disposition capabilities
Experience engaging with APS IT governance and Solution Front Door‑style architecture review processes
Awareness of emerging AI and Microsoft Copilot governance considerations for information management
Relevant qualifications in records management, information management, library science, law or a related discipline (desirable)

What you'll get in return

You'll have the opportunity to work on high-impact, nationally significant IM initiatives, with strong executive sponsorship and access to senior stakeholders. This role offers a chance to shape enduring policy and governance foundations, influence technology decisions, and apply best-practice information management in a modern Microsoft 365 environment.

What you need to do now

If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or email kylie.garcia@hay.com.au.

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

About the West Region Delivery Team

The West Region Delivery Team (WRDT) is delivering one of Sydney Water’s most significant infrastructure programs, with a circa $2bn pipeline of works over 10 years.

Operating in a collaborative, alliance-style environment, WRDT brings together delivery partners to plan and deliver critical water infrastructure that supports the growth of Greater Sydney.

About the opportunity

We are seeking a Data and Reporting Analyst to support the delivery of the WRDT program. Working closely with delivery, commercial and program leadership teams, you will interpret business requirements and translate them into meaningful reporting that improves visibility, supports decision making and drives better outcomes. Day to day you will;

Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports that provide clear, accurate and timely insights across the program.

Analyse data across cost, schedule, risk and delivery performance to identify trends, risks and opportunities.

Translate business requirements into structured, practical reporting outputs that support both operational and executive decision making.

Support the preparation of high-quality reports, presentations and executive-level slide decks.

Work closely with stakeholders to improve how data is captured, structured and used across the program.

Identify opportunities to streamline reporting processes and improve efficiency, consistency and usability.

Contribute to improving data quality and reporting frameworks across the program.

What you’ll bring

Strong experience in data analysis and reporting within a complex project or infrastructure environment.

Advanced Power BI capability, including dashboard development and data modelling.

Ability to interpret data and provide clear, practical insights, not just produce reports.

Strong commercial awareness, with an understanding of how data supports delivery outcomes.

Ability to engage with stakeholders and translate ambiguous requirements into structured outputs.

High attention to detail and ability to present information clearly and professionally.

Strong communication skills, with experience preparing executive-level presentations and reports.

Why join us

Be part of a long-term, high-profile infrastructure program with real impact.

A collaborative and supportive team environment.

Opportunities to grow and develop a long term career.

Interested? Apply Now!

If this sounds like you then APPLY NOW or reach out to Hayley Urriola for more information.

Job Title: Expression of Interest - Executive Advisor - Commercial, SA
Ad Text:
At GHD, we don’t just believe in the power of commitment, we live and breathe it every day.

Don’t let our size worry you. Although we are a large firm supporting 12,000+ employees across the world, we are an Australian company approaching 100 years in operation that is 100% employee owned. Our size ensures we have access to world-class talent, but our ownership structure ensures authentic and agile project delivery by local experts. 

Our GHD’s Infrastructure Advisory practice, a team of > 400 people across ANZ, South-east Asia and the Pacific, supports Government, asset managers and infrastructure investors who make informed decisions about capital deployment, operational optimisation and liability transformation across every industry sector.

Who are we looking for?

We are looking for Executive Advisors who are familiar with business case development, economic / financial modelling, project advisory, major project procurement modelling and strategy / regulation as we expand our Commercial Advisory team in SA.

Ideally based in Adelaide Office, your role will be to lead key Federal Government client relationships and engagements that shape Australia’s infrastructure policy and associated major projects.

Working with a high performing team this position offers a variety of work and will see you involved in:

Using your expertise and knowledge to successfully win, secure and deliver opportunities in business case development, economic modelling public policy and infrastructure strategy development.

Identify key contacts and opportunities and lead client engagement.

Leading project pursuits including internal teaming arrangements and contractual negotiations.

About Us

Workwear Group (WWG) is part of Wesfarmers Industrial & Safety a division of Wesfarmers, a top 20 ASX listed company with over 100,000 employees. Housed under the banner of Workwear Group, our three iconic brands - KingGee, Hard Yakka and NNT - have been in the business of delivering workwear since the late 1920's. 

We are the trusted partner in workwear solutions, manufacturing over seven million units each year. What sets us apart is our continuous investment in innovation, market knowledge, pioneering ideas and sustainability practices.

About the Role

As a Senior Integration Engineer, you will design, deliver, and maintain robust integration solutions that connect internal and external applications, with a strong focus on B2C and B2B e-commerce platforms. Leveraging Workato as the primary integration platform, alongside Microsoft Azure and other modern technologies, you’ll ensure seamless data flow, security, and scalability across critical business systems.

You’ll work closely with Product Owners, engineering teams, and business stakeholders to translate requirements into secure, high-performing solutions. This role demands expertise in API development, data transformation, and cloud integration, combined with strong problem-solving skills to optimize and troubleshoot complex integrations. Your work will underpin the digital backbone of our operations, enabling innovation and exceptional customer experiences.

What you’ll be doing

Design, develop, and maintain integration solutions using Workato and Azure technologies to connect internal and external systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders and technical teams to gather requirements and translate them into secure, scalable integration designs.
Build and manage APIs, ensuring compliance with authentication protocols and data security standards.
Monitor and optimize existing integrations for performance, reliability, and scalability.
Troubleshoot and resolve integration issues, including root cause analysis and implementing long-term fixes.
Ensure all incidents and service requests are resolved within agreed SLAs, maintaining clear communication with stakeholders.
Implement ETL processes and data transformation workflows to support accurate and efficient data exchange.
Participate in architecture reviews and present integration designs for approval and alignment with enterprise standards.
Support CI/CD pipelines and version control practices to enable continuous improvement and rapid deployment of integration solutions.
Provide after-hours support when required and maintain disaster recovery plans to ensure business continuity.
